mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 4.0% signals tightening labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain negative in 70%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in residential construction (HOUST) and real retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer demand that drives corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns show negative correlation with combined HOUST/RSAFS growth in 60%+ of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 12-month moving average signals weakening supply chain capacity that reduces inflationary pressures, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive in 70%+ of instances where INDPRO falls below its 12mo MA.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in CPI inflation above 2.5% triggers central bank rate hike expectations that raise equity discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain positive in 60%+ of instances where CPI exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) below 100k new jobs signals weakening labor demand that reduces corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns show positive correlation with PAYEMS declines in 50%+ of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in GDP (GDPC1) above 3% combined with rising housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong macroeconomic momentum that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns show negative correlation with combined GDPC1/HOUST growth in 50%+ of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate above 2.0% during a period of declining inflation (CPIAUCSL < 2.0%) signals a hawkish monetary policy that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive in 60%+ of instances where FEDFUNDS > 2.0% and CPI < 2.0%.
